Source_Species.reason: ScLDAP1 is described as a jojoba protein; jojoba is Simmondsia chinensis.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper identifies jojoba as Simmondsia chinensis in the title/abstract.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers list ScLDAP1 as Sc04g0010120.01.

Applied_Species.reason: ScLDAP1 was tested in N. benthamiana transient assays and in transgenic Arabidopsis seeds.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: These species are explicitly Nicotiana benthamiana and Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Arabidopsis experiments used WT Columbia (Col-0) and derived transgenic lines.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The central trait tested is packaging/storage of wax esters.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Wax esters are repeatedly described as neutral lipids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results show partitioning into lipid droplets and stable Arabidopsis seed experiments; both sites are supported.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper describes WEs as storage lipids and discusses engineering high-value non-native lipids/industrial relevance.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: ScLDAP1 is explicitly a LIPID DROPLET-ASSOCIATED PROTEIN; the paper also analyzes amphipathic α-helices in LDAP1.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: mCherry-ScLDAP1 localized specifically to LDs/LD monolayer surfaces.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show ScLDAP1 promotes WE partitioning from ER to LDs, improves LD morphology, increases seed WE in Arabidopsis lines, and partially restores germination.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point matches Results: unique rescue among jojoba LD proteins; increased WE recovery in LD fractions without total WE increase in transient leaves; improved seed morphology/WE/germination in Arabidopsis; helix 6 and L162 required/sufficient.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are directly used in the Results for ScLDAP1.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: ScLDAP1 function was shown to depend on NbLDIP, and prior work cited plus discussion note LDAP proteins interact with each other.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states jojoba LDAP1 is most abundantly expressed in developing seeds during WE accumulation and enriched in isolated LD proteome.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: ScLDAP1 was heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana leaves and Arabidopsis seeds.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These exact experimental contexts were used.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper reports deleterious effects from WE accumulation without proper packaging and only partial rescue by ScLDAP1; no explicit penalty of ScLDAP1 itself is reported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly frames ScLDAP1 as an important packaging factor for engineering non-native/high-value lipids in heterologous plants.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results show dependence on endogenous NbLDIP and species-specific distinction from At/Nb LDAP isoforms for WE packaging.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state ScLDAP1 in Arabidopsis constructs used the Brassica napus napin promoter; no transient promoter for ScLDAP1 is specified beyond vector context, so the phrasing 'transient expression promoter NA' is acceptable.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper explicitly cites prior proteomic/transcriptomic analyses identifying jojoba LDAPs enriched in WE-accumulating seed tissues; no accession for an omics dataset is given.

Source_Species.reason: NbLDIP is the endogenous N. benthamiana LDIP.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Explicit from gene name and accession section.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers list NbLDIP as Niben101Scf06413g00005.1.

Applied_Species.reason: NbLDIP was studied in N. benthamiana leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: NbLDIP was tested in the context of wax ester packaging.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Wax esters are described as neutral lipids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The tested phenotype concerns partitioning of WEs into lipid droplets.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Wax esters are treated as storage lipids/neutral lipids in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: LDIP is described as an Arabidopsis hydrophobic protein interacting with LDAPs and involved in LD biogenesis; NbLDIP is the homolog targeted here.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper discusses LDIP as interacting with LDAPs and SEIPINs in LD biogenesis, but does not directly localize NbLDIP in this study; LD/ER association is inferred, not shown here.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: RNAi suppression of NbLDIP reversed ScLDAP1-mediated correction of WE-induced ER defects, supporting requirement for efficient WE packaging.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Both listed findings are exactly the Results of Fig. 5.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: RNAi suppression, qualitative RT-PCR confirmation, confocal microscopy, and CFP-area quantification were used.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper states LDIP interacts with LDAPs and SEIPIN proteins in Arabidopsis, and tests functional dependence with ScLDAP1.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: NbLDIP is endogenous in N. benthamiana leaves and was suppressed there.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: NbLDIP RNAi suppression was applied during transient WE reconstitution assays.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Suppression reduced proper packaging/LD biogenesis behavior, as shown by restored ER defects.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Results support that LDIP compatibility matters, but 'sufficiency/compatibility should be considered' is an extrapolative engineering interpretation rather than directly tested.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper concludes ScLDAP1 function is LDIP-dependent and analogizes WE-LD formation to TAG-LD biogenesis.

Source_Species.reason: ScWS is identified as jojoba WS.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers list ScWS as Sc13g0002650.01.

Applied_Species.reason: ScWS was used in N. benthamiana transient assays and Arabidopsis transgenic seeds.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species are Nicotiana benthamiana and Arabidopsis thaliana.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: ScWS is used for wax ester production.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results show WE accumulation trapped in ER and partitioned into LDs in leaves, and accumulation in seeds of Arabidopsis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper describes jojoba WEs as storage lipids and discusses value as liquid wax/high-value lipids.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: ScWS is part of the reconstituted wax ester biosynthesis pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: As wax synthase, ScWS catalyzes the terminal assembly step to form WE.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Wax synthase is the terminal step forming the final WE from alcohol and acyl donor.

Enzyme_Name.reason: WS stands for wax synthase and is called jojoba WS in the Results.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Wax synthase function with fatty alcohol and acyl-CoA is directly implied by WE biosynthesis and standard enzyme role; the paper pairs WS with FAR to provide alcohol substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: ScWS contributes directly to formation of wax esters.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This is the established wax synthase reaction and is directly inferable from the paper's biosynthetic setup.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: ScWS directly forms the target product WE.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not establish this; 'Not established' is faithful.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Describing ScWS as wax synthase/acyltransferase is consistent with the enzyme identity, though family details are not elaborated.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: mCherry-ScWS localized to the ER with a normal reticulate ER pattern.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Co-expression with ScFAR drove WE production and associated ER defects when packaging was inadequate.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are shown in Results/Figs. 1 and 11.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods were used to assess ScWS-containing combinations.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: ScWS functionally acts with ScFAR and in context with ScLDAP1, but direct protein-protein interaction is not shown.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state ScWS used the soybean glycinin promoter in Arabidopsis seeds.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Transient expression at 4 dpi and stable seed expression are explicit.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: WE overaccumulation caused ER disruption, abnormal lipid structures, and reduced germination in seed systems.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: ScWS is a core engineering gene for WE production, as directly used here.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Transient assays showed ScLDAP1 did not increase total WE amount, separating ScWS-driven synthesis from packaging.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Methods state ScWS coding region/synthesized construct was based on jojoba seed cDNA/source.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: It was heterologously expressed in leaves and seeds.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers list ScFAR as Sc12g0004030.01.

Applied_Species.reason: This paper tests ScFAR in N. benthamiana transient assays, but stable Arabidopsis lines used MaFAR instead of ScFAR.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: ScFAR was applied in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: In N. benthamiana, WEs accumulated in ER defects and partially in LDs.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper treats WEs as storage lipids/high-value liquid waxes.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: ScFAR is one of the WE biosynthetic enzymes used to reconstitute the pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: ScFAR supplies fatty alcohol precursor for WE synthesis.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: It acts upstream of wax synthase.

Primary_Substrate.reason: FAR role as reducing fatty acyl-CoA to fatty alcohol is directly implied and standard for this enzyme; paper says ScFAE supports substrates for ScFAR and ScWS.

Primary_Product.reason: Fatty alcohol is the immediate FAR product, directly inferable from pathway logic.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This is the standard FAR reaction and is directly inferable from the WE pathway reconstruction.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: ScFAR forms the immediate precursor to WEs.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not establish this; 'Not established' is accurate.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Describing it as a fatty acyl reductase matches the gene identity.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: mCherry-ScFAR showed cytoplasmic localization; in coexpression assays ER defects occurred while ScFAR remained cytoplasmic.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: ScFAR with ScWS was required for WE synthesis and associated ER defects when packaging failed.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transient co-expression, localization, ESI-MS, and microscopy were used.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Functional cooperation with ScWS is shown, but direct physical interaction is not tested.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves was the tested context.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: In combination producing WEs, inadequate packaging led to deleterious ER phenotypes.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper uses FAR as an essential component of WE engineering and notes packaging factors should accompany biosynthetic genes.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly states MaFAR, not ScFAR, was used in Arabidopsis because of broader activity.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Methods indicate ScFAR was from jojoba seed cDNA/synthesized from jojoba sequences.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: ScFAR was heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.

corrections.reason: This paper tests ScFAR in N. benthamiana transient assays, but stable Arabidopsis lines used MaFAR instead of ScFAR.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers list ScFAE as Sc13g0004890.01.

Applied_Species.reason: ScFAE was used in N. benthamiana transient assays.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: ScFAE was included to support wax ester synthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: In the transient system, WEs are in ER/LD context; this field is broadly consistent with Results.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: ScFAE was used to elongate fatty acids to support WE biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: It functions in fatty acid elongation upstream of WE formation.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: It supplies upstream precursors before FAR/WS action.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper calls it jojoba FAE and uses it to elongate fatty acids, but does not specify the exact enzymatic subunit/name beyond FAE.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper states it elongates endogenous N. benthamiana fatty acids.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper explicitly says it elongates endogenous fatty acids to 20- and 22-carbon lengths.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This description matches the explicit statement in the Results.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: It supports precursor supply rather than directly making WE.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Calling it an 'FAE complex component' is plausible but not explicitly defined in this paper.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: ER localization is biologically likely for elongation, but not directly shown or stated for ScFAE here.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state ScFAE caused a slight, non-significant increase in WE formation and was included for optimal synthesis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Both points accurately reflect Fig. 1C and the associated text.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transient co-expression, ESI-MS, and wax species analysis are described.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Functional relation to ScFAR/ScWS is implied via substrate supply, but direct interactions are not shown.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: This was tested in transient leaf expression.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper uses ScFAE as a supportive substrate-optimization gene for heterologous WE production.

Other_Important_Info.reason: It was included subsequently for substrate optimization, not identified as the packaging determinant.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Methods say coding regions for ScFAE were isolated from jojoba seed cDNA.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: It was heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The Latin name Marinobacter aquaeolei is explicitly given.

Applied_Species.reason: MaFAR was used in transgenic Arabidopsis seeds.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: MaFAR was used with ScWS to produce wax esters.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Arabidopsis experiments concern seed lipids and LD morphology in embryos.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper treats WEs as storage lipids/high-value liquid waxes.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: MaFAR is part of the engineered WE biosynthesis pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: As FAR, it supplies fatty alcohols upstream of WS.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: It acts upstream of wax synthase.

Primary_Substrate.reason: A FAR reduces fatty acyl substrates to fatty alcohols; this is directly inferable and consistent with why it replaces ScFAR.

Primary_Product.reason: Fatty alcohols are the immediate FAR product, directly inferable from pathway role.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This is the standard FAR reaction and consistent with the paper's pathway description.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: MaFAR forms the immediate precursor to WEs.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Describing MaFAR as a fatty acyl reductase matches the paper.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: ScWS+MaFAR lines accumulated WEs and showed disrupted lipid packaging and poor germination, improved when ScLDAP1 was co-expressed.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Both points are explicitly stated in the transgenic Arabidopsis Results.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Stable transgenic seed expression, microscopy, WE/TAG quantification, and germination assays were used.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Functional context with ScWS and ScLDAP1 is shown, but direct interactions are not tested.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods state MaFAR was under the soybean β-conglycinin promoter.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The tested context was stable seed expression in Arabidopsis.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Wax ester production in these lines caused poor germination and aberrant lipid morphology without ScLDAP1.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper directly uses MaFAR as a broad-activity FAR for seed WE engineering.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Methods state the construct used YFP:myc:MaFAR in vector assembly.
